# File-Backed Array for Out-of-memory Computation

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dipterix/filearray/main/inst/hexbadge.png)

Stores large arrays in files to avoid occupying large memories.
Implemented with super fast gigabyte-level multi-threaded
reading/writing via `OpenMP`. Supports multiple non-character data types
(double, float, integer, complex, logical and raw).

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dipterix/filearray/main/adhoc/readme-speed.png)

Speed comparisons with `lazyarray` (`zstd`-compressed out-of-memory
array), and in-memory operation. The speed test was conducted on an
`MacBook Air (M1, 2020, 8GB RAM)`, with 8-threads. `filearray` is
uniformly faster than `lazyarray`. Random access has almost the same
speed as the native array operation in R. *(The actual speed may vary
depending on the storage type and memory size)*

## Installation

``` r
install.packages("filearray")
```

### Install Develop Version

The internal functions are written in `C++`. To avoid compiling the
packages, you can install from my personal repository. It’s
automatically updated every hour. Currently available on `Windows` and
`osx (Intel chip)` only.

``` r
options(repos = c(
    dipterix = 'https://dipterix.r-universe.dev',
    CRAN = 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))

install.packages('filearray')
```

Alternatively, you can compile from `Github` repository. This requires
proper compilers (`rtools` on `windows`, or `xcode-select --install` on
`osx`, or `build-essentials` on `linux`).

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("dipterix/filearray")
```

## Basic Usage

#### Create/load file array

``` r
library(filearray)
file <- tempfile()
x <- filearray_create(file, c(100, 100, 100, 100))

# load existing
x <- filearray_load(file)
```

See more:
[`help("filearray")`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)

#### Assign & subset array

``` r
x[,,,1] <- rnorm(1e6)
x[1:10,1,1,1]
```

#### Generics

``` r
typeof(x)
max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
apply(x, 3, min, na.rm = TRUE)

val = x[1,1,5,1]
fwhich(x, val, arr.ind = TRUE)
```

See more:
[`help("S3-filearray")`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S3-filearray.md),
[`help("fwhich")`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/fwhich.md)

#### Map-reduce

Process segments of array and reduce to save memories.

    # Identical to sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    mapreduce(x,
              map = \(data){ sum(data, na.rm = TRUE) },
              reduce = \(mapped){ do.call(sum, mapped) })

See more:
[`help("mapreduce")`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/mapreduce.md)

#### Collapse

Transform data, and collapse (calculate sum or mean) along margins.

    a <- x$collapse(keep = 4, method = "mean", transform = "asis")

    # equivalent to
    b <- apply(x[], 4, mean)

    a[1] - b[1]

Available `transform` for double/integer numbers are:

- `asis`: no transform
- `10log10`: `10 * log10(v)`
- `square`: `v * v`
- `sqrt`: `sqrt(v)`

For complex numbers, `transform` is a little bit different:

- `asis`: no transform
- `10log10`: `10 * log10(|x|^2)` (power to decibel unit)
- `square`: `|x|^2`
- `sqrt`: `|x|` (modulus)
- `normalize`: `x / |x|` (unit length)

## Notes

#### I. Notes on precision

1.  `complex` numbers: In native `R`, complex numbers are combination of
    two `double` numbers - real and imaginary (total 16 bytes). In
    `filearray`, complex numbers are coerced to two `float` numbers and
    store each number in 8 bytes. This conversion will gain performance
    speed, but lose precision at around 8 decimal place. For example,
    `1.0000001` will be store as `1`, or `123456789` will be stored as
    `123456792` (first 7 digits are accurate).

2.  `float` type: Native R does not have float type. All numeric values
    are stored in double precision. Since float numbers use half of the
    space, float arrays can be faster when hard drive speed is the
    bottle-neck (see [performance
    comparisons](https://dipterix.org/filearray/articles/performance.html)).
    However coercing double to float comes at costs: a). float number
    has less precision b). float number has smaller range
    ($3.4 \times 10^{38}$) than double ($1.7 \times 10^{308}$) hence use
    with caution when data needs high precision or the max is super
    large.

3.  `collapse` function: when data range is large (say `x[[1]]=1`, but
    `x[[2]]=10^20`), `collapse` method might lose precision. This is
    `double` only uses 8 bytes of memory space. When calculating
    summations, R internally uses `long double` to prevent precision
    loss, but current `filearray` implementation uses `double`, causing
    floating error around 16 decimal place.

#### II. Cold-start vs warm-start

As of version `0.1.1`, most file read/write operations are switched from
`fopen` to memory map for two simplify the logic (buffer size, kernel
cache…), and to boost the writing/some types of reading speed. While
sacrificing the speed of reading large block of data from 2.4GB/s to
1.7GB/s, the writing speed was boosted from 300MB/s to 700MB/s, and the
speed of random accessing small slices of data was increased from
900MB/s to 2.5GB/s. As a result, some functions can reach to really high
speed (close to in-memory calls) while using much less memory.

The additional performance improvements brought by the memory mapping
approach might be impacted by “cold” start. When reading/writing files,
most modern systems will cache the files so that it can load up these
files faster next time. I personally call it a cold start. Memory
mapping have a little bit extra overhead during the cold start,
resulting in decreased performance (but it’s still fast). Accessing the
same data after the cold start is called warm start. When operating with
warm starts, `filearray` is as fast as native R arrays (sometimes even
faster due to the indexing method and fewer garbage collections). This
means `filearray` reaches its best performance when the arrays are
re-used.

#### III. Using traditional HDD?

`filearray` relies on `SSD`, especially `NVMe SSD` that allows you to
fast-access random hard disk address. If you use `HDD`, `filearray` can
provide very limited improvement.

If you use `filearray` to direct access to HDD, please set number of
threads to `1` via `filearray::filearray_threads(1)` at start up, or set
system environment `FILEARRAY_NUM_THREADS` to `"1"`.

- [`FileArray-class`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/FileArray-class.md)
  : Definition of file array

  [`` `<=`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<complex>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `<=`( ``*`<complex>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `<=`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<logical>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `<=`( ``*`<logical>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `<=`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<array>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `<=`( ``*`<array>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<numeric>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<numeric>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<complex>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<complex>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<logical>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<logical>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<array>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `&`( ``*`<array>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<numeric>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<numeric>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<complex>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<complex>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<logical>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<logical>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`,`*`<array>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `|`( ``*`<array>`*`,`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`` `!`( ``*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`exp(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`expm1(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`log(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`log10(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`log2(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`log1p(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`abs(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`sqrt(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`sign(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`signif(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`trunc(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`floor(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`ceiling(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`round(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`acos(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`acosh(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`asin(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`asinh(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`atan(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`atanh(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`cos(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`cosh(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`cospi(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`sin(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`sinh(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`sinpi(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`tan(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`tanh(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`tanpi(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`gamma(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`lgamma(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`digamma(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`trigamma(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`Arg(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`Conj(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`Im(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`Mod(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`Re(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  [`is.na(`*`<FileArray>`*`)`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/S4-filearray.md)
  :

  'S4' methods for `FileArray`

- [`apply()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/apply.md) : Apply
  functions over file array margins (extended)

- [`as_filearray()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)
  [`as_filearrayproxy()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)
  [`filearray_create()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)
  [`filearray_load()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)
  [`filearray_checkload()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)
  [`filearray_load_or_create()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray.md)
  : Create or load existing file arrays

- [`filearray_bind()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray_bind.md)
  : Merge and bind homogeneous file arrays

- [`filearray_threads()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/filearray_threads.md)
  : Set or get file array threads

- [`fmap()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/fmap.md)
  [`fmap2()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/fmap.md)
  [`fmap_element_wise()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/fmap.md)
  : Map multiple file arrays and save results

- [`fwhich()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/fwhich.md) :

  A generic function of `which` that is `'FileArray'` compatible

- [`mapreduce()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/mapreduce.md)
  : A map-reduce method to iterate blocks of file-array data with little
  memory usage

- [`typeof()`](https://dipterix.org/filearray/reference/typeof.md) : The
  type of a file array (extended)

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Performance Comparisons -
  (Numerical)](https://dipterix.org/filearray/articles/performance.md):
